body:
### Problem

Some recent Nvim commit will make the command `:UnnestEdit` in plugin [unnest.nvim](https://github.com/brianhuster/unnest.nvim) crash Nvim. But it didn't crash on Nvim nightly just 9 days ago (https://github.com/brianhuster/unnest.nvim/actions/runs/22768406576)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create file `repro.lua` with the following content
```lua
local api = vim.api
local child_chan = vim.fn.jobstart({
	"nvim",
	"--clean",
	"+lua parent = vim.fn.sockconnect('pipe', vim.env.NVIM, {rpc = true})",
	"+lua vim.fn.rpcnotify(parent, 'nvim_cmd', {cmd = 'edit', args = {'README.md'}}, {})"
}, {
	term = true,
})

vim.cmd.startinsert()

local buf = api.nvim_get_current_buf()
api.nvim_create_autocmd("BufHidden", {
	buffer = buf,
	callback = function()
		vim.fn.jobstop(child_chan)
		vim.schedule(function()
			api.nvim_buf_delete(buf, { force = true })
		end)
	end,
})
```
2. Run it with `nvim --clean --headless -S repro.lua`
3. Nvim will crash. Below is backtrace
```gdb

  <https://debuginfod.ubuntu.com>

To make this setting permanent, add 'set debuginfod enabled off' to .gdbinit.

Using host libthread_db library "/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libthread_db.so.1".
[Detaching after fork from child process 404012]
-- TERMINAL --
Program received signal SIGSEGV, Segmentation fault.
marktree_lookup (b=0x30d8, id=18446744065119617024, itr=0x7fffffffcf90) at /media/brianhuster/D/neovim/src/nvim/marktree.c:2196
2196	  MTNode *n = id2node(b, id);
#0  marktree_lookup (b=0x30d8, id=18446744065119617024, itr=0x7fffffffcf90) at /media/brianhuster/D/neovim/src/nvim/marktree.c:2196
        n = <optimized out>
        i = <optimized out>
#1  0x0000555555728c26 in marktree_lookup_ns (itr=0x7fffffffcf90, end=false, id=<optimized out>, ns=<optimized out>, b=0x30d8) at /media/brianhuster/D/neovim/src/nvim/marktree.c:2150
No locals.
#2  extmark_del_id (buf=0x0, ns_id=<optimized out>, id=<optimized out>) at /media/brianhuster/D/neovim/src/nvim/extmark.c:156
        itr = {{pos = {row = 0, col = 0}, lvl = 0, x = 0x0, i = 0, s = {{oldcol = 0, i = 0} <repeats 20 times>}, intersect_idx = 0, intersect_pos = {row = 0, col = 0}, intersect_pos_x = {row = 0, col = 0}}}
        key = <optimized out>

        term = 0x555555cc8740

        prev_oap = 0x0

        fname = <optimized out>
        params = {argc = 5, argv = 0x7fffffffdb08, use_vimrc = 0x555555aa33b5 "NONE", clean = true, n_commands = 1, commands = {0x555555c38750 "\"", 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0}, cmds_tofree = "\001\000\000\000\000\000\000\000\000", n_pre_commands = 0, pre_commands = {0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0}, luaf = 0x0, lua_arg0 = -1, edit_type = 0, tagname = 0x0, use_ef = 0x0, input_istext = false, no_swap_file = 0, use_debug_break_level = -1, window_count = 1, window_layout = 0, diff_mode = 0, listen_addr = 0x0, remote = 0, server_addr = 0x0, scriptin = 0x0, scriptout = 0x0, scriptout_append = false, had_stdin_file = false}
        cwd = 0x0

        remote_ui = <optimized out>
        use_remote_ui = <optimized out>
        listen_and_embed = <optimized out>
        vimrc_none = <optimized out>
        __PRETTY_FUNCTION__ = "main"
```

### Expected behavior

Does not crash

### Nvim version (nvim -v)

NVIM v0.12.0-dev-0ca9849387 Build type: RelWithDebInfo LuaJIT 2.1.1772619647

### Vim (not Nvim) behaves the same?

NA

### Operating system/version

Kubuntu 24.04.1

### Terminal name/version

NA

### $TERM environment variable

NA

### Installation

from source

comment_data.body:
looks like `nvim_buf_delete` deleted the buffer before extmark could be deleted.

Sorry! I wasn't aware of this scenario, will add `if (buf)` check before deleting extmark
For more info, it doesn't crash if I just remove the line `vim.cmd.startinsert()`. But I can not remove that line from my plugin, as it is intended
related https://github.com/neovim/neovim/discussions/38315#discussioncomment-16145681 : can we move that logic to a Lua `TermClose` handler?

labels.description: issue reporting a crash or segfault
labels.name: bug-crash

title: crash in terminal.c .. `extmark_del_id`
